title: End-to-End Optimized Image Compression
key_idea: Nonlinear transform coding with GDN and differentiable quantization surrogate.
reported_result: Foundational end-to-end learned transform codec.
caveats: Historical baseline; implementation details and entropy coder differ from modern frameworks.

title: Variational Image Compression with a Scale Hyperprior
key_idea: Side-information hyperprior predicts spatially varying latent scales.
reported_result: Established hyperprior as a standard LIC component.
caveats: Compare actual bitstreams and color preprocessing carefully.

title: Joint Autoregressive and Hierarchical Priors for Learned Image Compression
key_idea: Combines hyperprior and autoregressive spatial context.
reported_result: Improved entropy estimates over either prior alone.
caveats: Sequential decoding cost is central.

title: Learned Image Compression with Discretized Gaussian Mixture Likelihoods and Attention Modules
key_idea: Gaussian-mixture entropy model and attention-enhanced transforms.
reported_result: Widely used strong convolutional baseline.
caveats: CompressAI implementation may not be byte-identical to author code.

title: ELIC: Efficient Learned Image Compression with Unevenly Grouped Space-Channel Contextual Adaptive Coding
key_idea: Uneven channel groups and space-channel context for efficiency and progressive decoding.
reported_result: Strong speed/RD trade-off and common baseline.
caveats: Frequently used code is a reimplementation; label accordingly.

title: The Devil Is in the Details: Window-based Attention for Image Compression
key_idea: Window attention and entropy modeling tailored to LIC.
reported_result: Representative early Transformer LIC.
caveats: Official implementation availability must be rechecked before use.

title: Learned Image Compression with Mixed Transformer-CNN Architectures
key_idea: Parallel Transformer-CNN mixture blocks and channel-wise entropy model.
reported_result: Strong hybrid backbone baseline.
caveats: Check training corpus and exact VTM reference.

title: MLIC: Multi-Reference Entropy Model for Learned Image Compression
datasets: DIV2K; Flickr2K; CLIC; COCO; ImageNet; Kodak; Tecnick
key_idea: Joint channel, local spatial and global references.
reported_result: Author reports Kodak BD-rate -8.05% for MLIC and -11.39% for MLIC+ vs VTM-17.0.
caveats: Use exact model generation and actual stream path.

title: MLIC++: Linear Complexity Multi-Reference Entropy Modeling for Learned Image Compression
key_idea: Linear-complexity local/global multi-reference entropy model.
reported_result: Author reports Kodak/Tecnick/CLIC Pro BD-rate -13.39/-17.59/-13.08% vs VTM-17.0.
caveats: Repository notes architecture/checkpoint corrections; pin commit and model class.

title: Frequency-aware Transformer for Learned Image Compression
datasets: OpenImages or author training corpus; Kodak; Tecnick; CLIC
key_idea: Frequency-decomposition window attention and frequency modulation.
reported_result: Author reports double-digit BD-rate gains over VTM on standard sets.
caveats: Verify exact paper URL and training corpus from pinned README/paper before citation.

title: Learned Image Compression with Dictionary-based Entropy Model
key_idea: Learnable external dictionary supplies training-set priors to cross-attention entropy model.
reported_result: Reported state-of-the-art rate/latency balance.
caveats: README does not fully pin dependency versions; create local lock.

title: Linear Attention Modeling for Learned Image Compression
key_idea: Bi-RWKV transform and RWKV spatial-channel context.
reported_result: Author reports approximately -15% to -17% BD-rate vs VTM-9.1 depending on set/version.
caveats: CUDA operator compatibility is a practical risk; pinned snapshot removed hard T_MAX.

title: MambaIC: State Space Models for High-Performance Learned Image Compression
key_idea: State-space transform/context with local attention.
reported_result: High-resolution efficiency focus.
caveats: Repository explicitly says it is not exact original code; training data differs from some competitors.

title: MLICv2: Enhanced Multi-Reference Entropy Modeling for Learned Image Compression
key_idea: Token mixing, hyperprior-guided global correlation, channel reweighting and instance adaptation.
reported_result: Author reports MLICv2+ BD-rate around -20.46/-24.35/-19.14% vs VTM-17.0.
caveats: Confirm whether complete MLICv2 training/evaluation code is present in the selected snapshot.

title: Adaptive Learned Image Compression with Graph Neural Networks
key_idea: Dual-scale content-adaptive graph and variable neighbor count.
reported_result: Author reports -19.29/-21.69/-18.71% BD-rate vs VTM-9.1.
caveats: Code was announced; verify completeness/checkpoints at use time.

title: HiDE: Hierarchical Dictionary-Based Entropy Modeling for Learned Image Compression
key_idea: Hierarchical global/local dictionaries and multi-receptive-field parameter estimation.
reported_result: Preprint reports -18.50/-21.99/-24.01% BD-rate vs VTM-12.1.
caveats: Not peer-reviewed at snapshot; code unconfirmed.

title: Block-based Learned Image Compression without Blocking Artifacts
datasets: 4K test images; four CNN LIC models
metrics: RD equivalence; peak memory; peak compute
key_idea: Analytically derive minimum overlap for artifact-free tiled inference without retraining.
reported_result: Reports average peak memory reduced to 13.94% encoder and 13.33% decoder of full-image processing.
caveats: Primarily CNN-based; applicability to global attention/SSM must be tested.

title: What Matters in Practical Learned Image Compression
metrics: human preference; bitrate savings; mobile latency
key_idea: Joint perceptual/runtime design and performance-aware NAS.
reported_result: Reports 2.3–3x bitrate savings in subjective tests and 230/150 ms 12MP encode/decode on iPhone 17 Pro Max.
caveats: Subjective protocol and implementation access are essential for reproduction.

title: Rethinking Lossy Compression: The Rate-Distortion-Perception Tradeoff
key_idea: Formalizes incompatibility between perfect perception and minimal distortion at fixed rate.
reported_result: Theoretical basis for perceptual compression evaluation.
caveats: Distributional realism does not guarantee instance-level text correctness.

title: High-Fidelity Generative Image Compression
metrics: FID; LPIPS; PSNR; user study; bpp
key_idea: Adversarial perceptual decoder with explicit rate control.
reported_result: Canonical GAN-based high-fidelity compression.
caveats: GAN texture can alter text and exact symbols.

title: Multi-Realism Image Compression with a Conditional Generator
metrics: rate; distortion; perceptual metrics; realism control
key_idea: Single representation supports adjustable realism at decoder.
reported_result: Demonstrates a continuum between distortion and realism without new bitstream.
caveats: Decoder control may change text semantics.

title: Lossy Image Compression with Conditional Diffusion Models
key_idea: Separate transmitted content latent from generated texture variables.
reported_result: Strong perceptual quality with few-step conditional diffusion variants.
caveats: Sampling latency and stochastic semantic changes.

title: Towards Image Compression with Perfect Realism at Ultra-Low Bitrates
key_idea: VQ local representation plus global image description conditions a diffusion prior.
reported_result: Targets rates down to about 0.003 bpp.
caveats: Original foundation model/code not fully open; not suitable as sole reproducibility baseline.

title: PerCo (SD): Open Perceptual Compression
key_idea: Open Stable Diffusion implementation of PerCo-style compression.
reported_result: Open competitive alternative with fidelity/perception tradeoff.
caveats: Verify current weight release and exact license.

title: Generative Latent Coding for Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression
key_idea: VQ-VAE latent, categorical hyperprior and code prediction supervision.
reported_result: Reports comparable FID to MS-ILLM with materially fewer bits on CLIC2020.
caveats: Perceptual distribution metrics cannot substitute OCR correctness.

title: PICD: Versatile Perceptual Image Compression with Diffusion Rendering
datasets: WebUI training; SCI1K; SIQAD; natural image sets
metrics: text Jaccard/accuracy; FID; LPIPS; DISTS; bpp; runtime
key_idea: Separately encode text and image, then diffusion-render screen content.
reported_result: Strong text and perceptual quality for screen images.
caveats: Text/position side-channel, OCR failure dependency, privacy and slow diffusion must be fully accounted.

title: Differentiable Vector Quantization for Rate-Distortion Optimization of Generative Image Compression
key_idea: Differentiable codebook-distribution relaxation enables entropy-constrained VQ and test-time rate control.
reported_result: Reports up to 75.71% bitrate reduction at matched DISTS and 37.63% at matched LPIPS vs RDEIC on DIV2K-val.
caveats: Repository license and third-party tokenizer assets require review.

title: CoD: A Diffusion Foundation Model for Image Compression
metrics: FID; LPIPS; DISTS; PSNR; bpp; training cost
key_idea: Compression-oriented diffusion foundation model trained from scratch rather than adapting text-to-image models.
reported_result: Reports particularly strong results near 0.0039 bpp and much lower training cost than Stable Diffusion pretraining.
caveats: Large decoder and generative semantic fidelity remain concerns for text.

title: CADC: Content Adaptive Diffusion-Based Generative Image Compression
key_idea: Uncertainty-guided adaptive quantization, auxiliary information concentration and bitrate-free textual conditioning from reconstruction.
reported_result: Reports state-of-the-art perceptual quality at ultra-low rates.
caveats: "Bitrate-free" conditioning is decoder-derived but its computation and possible semantic bias still matter.

title: Ultra-Low Bitrate Perceptual Image Compression with Shallow Encoder
key_idea: Asymmetric shallow encoder with one-step diffusion decoder and dual-side distillation.
reported_result: Reports 35.8 FPS encoding for 1080p images.
caveats: Evaluate exact sequence fidelity before using for text.

title: VLIC: Vision-Language Models as Perceptual Judges for Human-Aligned Image Compression
key_idea: Post-train diffusion codec with zero-shot VLM binary judgments.
reported_result: Reports competitive human-aligned performance.
caveats: VLM judge bias and inability to enforce exact text semantics.

title: Recognition-Aware Learned Image Compression
key_idea: Joint rate-distortion and recognition objective.
reported_result: Reports large low-rate classification gains over traditional codecs in its setup.
caveats: Task-specific and classification-centric.

title: TransTIC: Transferring Transformer-based Image Compression from Human Perception to Machine Perception
key_idea: Instance-specific encoder prompts and task-specific decoder prompts adapt a frozen Transformer codec.
reported_result: Strong transfer without full codec fine-tuning.
caveats: Prompt/side information rate and task-model dependence require care.

title: Rate-Distortion-Cognition Controllable Versatile Neural Image Compression
key_idea: Single model controls rate, reconstruction distortion and cognition with scalable residual branch.
reported_result: Flexible Pareto control across machine tasks.
caveats: OCR sequence integrity is not explicitly modeled.

title: Test-Time Fine-Tuning of Image Compression Models for Multi-Task Adaptability
key_idea: Instance-specific SVD-LoRA adaptation with singular-value side information.
reported_result: Improves adaptability to unseen machine tasks.
caveats: Test-time compute and adapter bitrate must be counted.

title: Bridging Compressed Image Latents and Multimodal Large Language Models
key_idea: Lightweight transform neck and surrogate visual-encoder loss connect codec latents to MLLMs.
reported_result: Avoids full MLLM in codec training loop.
caveats: Paper URL and exact model versions should be recorded from repo before citation.

title: Deep Image Coding for Machines with Semantic-Guided Self-Supervision
key_idea: Compressed-domain multi-task representation with semantic-guided self-supervision.
reported_result: Designed for machine consumption without full RGB reconstruction.
caveats: Verify definitive title/venue from the pinned repository.

title: Towards Unified Human Perception and Machine Understanding: Token Flow Guided Compression Framework
metrics: LVLM task scores; perceptual quality; bpp
key_idea: Variable-length 1D visual tokens, token-flow propagation and direct LVLM consumption.
reported_result: Reports strong LVLM understanding at comparable rates.
caveats: OCR exact-sequence preservation remains a distinct requirement.

title: JPEG AI — ISO/IEC 6048-1:2025 / ITU-T T.840.1 Core Coding System
metrics: subjective quality; objective metrics; machine tasks; conformance
key_idea: First end-to-end learning-based international image coding standard; compact single stream, progressive, ROI and machine-consumption scope.
reported_result: Official overview reports about 30% rate reduction at equal subjective quality relative to conventional standards.
caveats: Reference software, profile and conformance version must be pinned; standards/patent terms differ from code license.

title: Deep Image Compression Using Scene Text Quality Assessment
datasets: scene text quality and compression datasets
metrics: quality prediction MAE/correlation; subjective/objective text quality
key_idea: Scene-text image quality assessor guides iterative variable-rate compression.
reported_result: Reports better text quality and quality-assessment accuracy than compared regressors.
caveats: Iterative, assessor and codec are not a unified end-to-end utility allocation system.

title: Learned Image Compression with Text Quality Enhancement
key_idea: Text logit loss aligns OCR outputs between original and reconstruction.
reported_result: Reports average BD-rate -32.64% at fixed CER and -28.03% at fixed WER.
caveats: Single OCR dependence, screenshot focus and cross-OCR transfer need evaluation.

title: TFIC: End-to-End Text-Focused Image Compression for Coding for Machines
datasets: approximately 20k synthetic train and small synthetic test reported
key_idea: Frozen OCR supplies sequence supervision to a Transformer codec.
reported_result: Reports very large OCR gains in selected very-low-rate settings and encoder faster than OCR.
caveats: Small synthetic evaluation, single OCR and no strong cross-OCR evidence.

title: MALICE: Manipulation Attacks on Learned Image Compression
datasets: multiple LIC models and image sets
key_idea: Adversarial inputs induce bitrate or reconstruction failures.
reported_result: Reports severe white-box and measurable black-box bitrate amplification.
caveats: Threat model and codec versions must be matched.

title: Backdoor Attacks Against Deep Image Compression via Adaptive Frequency Trigger
key_idea: Frequency-adaptive trigger preserves clean performance while activating compression failure.
reported_result: Demonstrates supply-chain/backdoor risk in learned codecs.
caveats: Locate exact CVF paper URL before formal citation.

title: Adversarial Attacks on Learned Image Compression: Rate-Distortion Vulnerability Analysis
key_idea: Separates rate and distortion attacks and diagnoses hyperprior/IGDN vulnerabilities.
reported_result: Shows broad vulnerability across architectures.
caveats: Use actual bitstream rate, not likelihood only, when reproducing.

title: Towards Robust Learned Image Compression: Benchmarking Common Corruptions
key_idea: Corruption benchmark for LIC robustness.
reported_result: Provides standardized corruption evaluation.
caveats: Confirm final publication status/version.

title: Robust Neural Image Compression
key_idea: Adversarial training/robustification for neural image compression.
reported_result: Public implementation useful as defense baseline.
caveats: Verify exact paper metadata from repository.

title: Bitstream Collision Attacks on Learned Image Compression
key_idea: Construct semantically distinct inputs that map to the same or colliding bitstream representation.
reported_result: Raises authenticity and semantic-integrity concerns.
caveats: Preprint; inspect exact threat constraints.

title: Invertible Image Signal Processing
metrics: RAW reconstruction; RGB quality; JPEG robustness
key_idea: Invertible RAW↔sRGB ISP with differentiable JPEG simulation.
reported_result: Supports RAW recovery from rendered/compressed RGB.
caveats: Not a pure learned bitstream codec; camera-specific data matter.

title: RAWtoBit: A Fully End-to-End Camera ISP and Image Compression Framework
key_idea: Jointly optimize RAW-to-RGB ISP and compression with dual-teacher distillation.
reported_result: Improves over separately optimized ISP and codec pipelines.
caveats: Sensor generalization and data pairing are major limitations.

title: Raw Image Reconstruction with Learned Compact Metadata
metrics: RAW reconstruction; metadata rate; RGB quality
key_idea: Transmit compact learned metadata alongside sRGB for RAW recovery.
reported_result: Scalable representation for display RGB and recoverable RAW.
caveats: All metadata rate must be included.

title: ρ-Vision: Direct RAW Image Compression and Computer Vision
key_idea: Direct RAW compression and machine vision with unpaired RAW simulation.
reported_result: Includes MultiRAW dataset and code.
caveats: License and camera-data terms must be checked.

title: ParamISP: Learned Forward and Inverse ISPs using Camera Parameters
key_idea: Condition ISP on ISO, exposure, aperture and focal parameters.
reported_result: Improves sensor/capture adaptation.
caveats: Metadata availability and normalization vary by camera.

title: CoDISP: Exploring Compressed-Domain Camera ISP with RGB-Guided Encoder
key_idea: Perform ISP operations in compressed domain with shared latent.
reported_result: Targets mobile memory and integrated ISP processing.
caveats: Workshop result; evaluate against current full pipelines.

title: Multi-Modality Deep Network for Extreme Learned Image Compression
datasets: captioned image datasets; verify exact splits from paper
metrics: LPIPS; FID; KID; PSNR; MS-SSIM; user study; total bitrate
key_idea: Inject transmitted text semantics into encoder, entropy model and decoder through cross-modal attention/complement modules and semantic-consistency loss.
reported_result: Author reports comparable or better perceptual results than prior methods operating at roughly 2x–4x the bitrate.
caveats: Caption side information changes the coding problem; count compressed text and all metadata, and do not treat semantic plausibility as OCR preservation.

title: Neural Image Compression with Text-guided Encoding for both Pixel-level and Perceptual Fidelity
datasets: MS-COCO 30k and additional paper datasets; verify exact splits
key_idea: Use text-adaptive image encoding and joint image-text training while avoiding a text-conditioned generative decoder.
reported_result: Author reports the best LPIPS among compared baselines while retaining competitive pixel fidelity.
caveats: Human/machine captions may be side information; reproduce with full caption-rate accounting and exact caption-generation policy.

title: LMM-driven Semantic Image-Text Coding for Ultra Low-bitrate Learned Image Compression
datasets: Kodak and paper-specific low-rate evaluation sets; verify exact split
metrics: LPIPS; LPIPS BD-rate; PSNR; MS-SSIM; bitrate
key_idea: Generate and compress captions inside an LMM-assisted image-text coding pipeline and fine-tune an LIC network with semantic-perceptual objectives.
reported_result: Author reports 41.58% LPIPS BD-rate improvement over compared methods.
caveats: Depends on LMM, caption coder and restoration model; pin every weight and count caption bytes, prompts and container overhead.

title: SELIC: Semantic-Enhanced Learned Image Compression via High-Level Textual Guidance
metrics: PSNR; MS-SSIM; BD-rate; encode/decode time; parameters
key_idea: Use frozen BLIP and BERT branches to derive semantic features and fuse them with image latents before quantization and entropy coding.
reported_result: Author reports about 0.1–0.15 dB gain over its non-semantic baseline and -4.9% BD-rate versus VVC on Kodak.
caveats: Encoder invokes image-to-text/text encoders; verify whether all semantic influence is represented in the serialized latent and reproduce timing/rate under a matched codec protocol.
